20	## Overview
21	
22	DDx needs to discover, install, and manage resources from an online library. The library is a git repository (`ddx-library`) containing personas, prompts, templates, patterns, MCP configurations, and **package descriptors** for workflow tools like HELIX. DDx fetches resources on demand — no git subtree, no full repo clone.
23	
24	This replaces the current git-subtree-based sync model with a lighter, more practical approach: DDx downloads what you ask for, caches it locally, and keeps track of what's installed.
25	
26	## Problem Statement
27	
28	**Current situation:**
29	- `ddx-library` exists as a git repo but DDx's sync mechanism (`ddx update`) is a stub
30	- Git subtree is complex, fragile, and overkill for distributing personas and templates
31	- HELIX publishes skills independently (global home paths) with no DDx integration
32	- External check runners expect plugins at `~/.cache/ddx/library/plugins/` but nothing populates this path
33	- There's no way to discover what's available or install a specific resource
34	
35	**Desired outcome:** `ddx install helix` fetches and installs HELIX skills. `ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer` fetches one persona. `ddx search testing` finds testing-related resources. Simple, practical, no git subtree.
36	
37	## Architecture
38	
39	### Registries
40	
41	DDx supports multiple registries. Each registry is a git repository containing a `registry.yaml` index and installable resources. Registries are checked in order — first match wins.

171	### Non-Functional
172	
173	- **No git subtree** — fetch individual files or shallow clones, not full repo history

178	## CLI Commands
179	
180	```bash
181	ddx update                          # Refresh registry from upstream
182	ddx search <query>                  # Search available resources
183	ddx install <name>                  # Install a resource or package
184	ddx install helix                   # Install HELIX workflow
185	ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer  # Install one persona
186	ddx installed                       # List what's installed
187	ddx outdated                        # Check for available updates
188	ddx upgrade <name>                  # Update a package to latest version
189	ddx uninstall <name>                # Remove an installed resource
190	```
191	
192	## User Stories
193	
194	### US-090: Developer Discovers Available Workflows
195	**As a** developer evaluating DDx
196	**I want** to search for available workflow tools
197	**So that** I can find and install HELIX or other methodologies
198	
199	**Acceptance Criteria:**
200	- Given I run `ddx search workflow`, then I see HELIX and any other registered workflows with descriptions
201	- Given I run `ddx install helix`, then HELIX skills are installed to `.agents/skills/` (project-local; FEAT-015) and the CLI to `~/.local/bin/`
202	
203	### US-091: Developer Installs Individual Resources
204	**As a** developer customizing my project
205	**I want** to install specific personas or templates from the library
206	**So that** I get exactly what I need without bulk downloading
207	
208	**Acceptance Criteria:**
209	- Given I run `ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer`, then the persona file is copied to `.ddx/library/personas/`
210	- Given I run `ddx installed`, then I see `persona/strict-code-reviewer` with version and install date
211	
212	## Migration from Git Subtree
213	
214	The current `ddx init` creates a git subtree for `.ddx/library/`. This should be replaced:
215	
216	1. **New projects:** `ddx init` creates `.ddx/library/` as a local directory (no subtree)
217	2. **Library population:** `ddx install` fetches resources on demand
218	3. **Existing projects:** The git subtree continues to work but is no longer the recommended flow
219	4. **`ddx contribute`:** Remains as a way to push changes back (creates a PR against ddx-library)
220	
221	## Dependencies
222	
223	- `ddx-library` repo with `registry.yaml`
224	- GitHub API or git archive for fetching individual files
225	- Package descriptors in workflow tool repos (HELIX, etc.)
226	
227	## Out of Scope
228	
229	- Package signing or verification (v2)

25	## D1 — Policy: library vs project content
26	
27	Personas can live in two places:
28	
29	- **Library personas** — `<library>/personas/*.md`. Shipped with DDx, shared
30	  across projects, pulled via `ddx update`, contributed upstream via
31	  `ddx contribute`. **Read-only in UI and API.** Edits are refused with a
32	  typed error; the only mutation affecting library content is `fork`, which
33	  copies the file into the project.
34	
35	- **Project-local personas** — `.ddx/personas/*.md` under the project root.
36	  Live with the project in its VCS. Fully editable via UI, GraphQL, and CLI.
37	  Never pushed upstream by `ddx contribute`.
38	
39	### Override rule
40	
41	When a project-local persona has the same name as a library persona, the
42	project-local file wins for that project's bindings and list views. List
43	views expose one effective persona per name; the source badge becomes
44	`project` for an override so the reader can see that project-local content
45	is active. Load order: project first, then library; duplicate names collapse
46	to the project-local file.
47	
48	### What this does not decide
49	
50	- Multi-project / org-level persona sharing — out of scope.
51	- Rich in-browser markdown editor — a plain `<textarea>` is enough for v1.
52	- Upstreaming project personas — still happens via `ddx contribute`.
53	- Changing how personas get injected into agent prompts — server read path
54	  is unchanged.

137	// refreshShippedSkills re-copies the embedded `ddx` skill into the project's
138	// skill directories and refreshes the AGENTS.md DDx block. Safe to call on
139	// every `ddx update` because skills.Install with Force=true handles the
140	// "existing files should be updated" case, and generateAgentsMD's
141	// marker-based merge is idempotent. Stale pre-consolidation skill dirs
142	// (ddx-bead, ddx-run, etc.) are swept by cleanupBootstrapSkills.
143	func refreshShippedSkills(workingDir string) {
144		bootstrapSkillNames := []string{"ddx"}
145		for _, dir := range []string{
146			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "skills"),
147			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".agents", "skills"),
148			filepath.Join(workingDir, ".claude", "skills"),
149		} {
150			_ = os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755)
151			cleanupBootstrapSkills(dir, bootstrapSkillNames)
152		}
153		if err := skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, workingDir, skills.Options{Force: true}); err != nil {
154			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: skill install failed: %v\n", err)
155		}
156		registerBootstrapDDxSkills(workingDir, true)
157		generateAgentsMD(workingDir)
158	}

161	func extractUpdateOptions(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) (*UpdateOptions, error) {
162		opts := &UpdateOptions{}
163	
164		// Extract flags
165		opts.Check, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("check")
166		opts.Force, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
167		opts.Reset, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("reset")
168		opts.Sync, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("sync")
169		opts.Strategy, _ = cmd.Flags().GetString("strategy")
170		opts.Backup, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("backup")
171		opts.Interactive, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("interactive")
172		opts.Abort, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("abort")
173		opts.DryRun, _ = cmd.Flags().GetBool("dry-run")
174	
175		// Handle mine/theirs flags by converting to strategy
176		updateMine, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("mine")
177		updateTheirs, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("theirs")
178	
179		if updateMine && updateTheirs {
180			return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot use both --mine and --theirs flags")
181		}
182		if updateMine {
183			opts.Strategy = "ours"
184		}

187		}
188

190		if len(args) > 0 {
191			opts.Resource = args[0]
192		}

194		return opts, nil

197	// Output formatting function
198	func displayUpdateResult(cmd *cobra.Command, result *UpdateResult, opts *UpdateOptions) error {
199		green := color.New(color.FgGreen)
200		red := color.New(color.FgRed)
201	
202		out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
203		writer := out.(io.Writer)
204	
205		// Handle error cases
206		if !result.Success {
207			if len(result.Conflicts) > 0 {
208				return handleConflictOutput(out, result.Conflicts, opts)
209			}
210			_, _ = red.Fprintln(writer, "❌", result.Message)
211			return nil
212		}
213	
214		// Handle check mode
215		if opts.Check {
216			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Checking for updates...")
217			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Fetching latest changes from master repository...")
218			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Available updates:")
219			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "Changes since last update:")
220			return nil
221		}
222	
223		// Handle dry-run mode
224		if opts.DryRun {
225			return displayDryRunResult(out, result, opts)
226		}
227	
228		// Display success message
229		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "✅", result.Message)
230		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
231	
232		// Show updated files
233		if len(result.UpdatedFiles) > 0 {
234			_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "📦 Updated resources:")
235			for _, file := range result.UpdatedFiles {
236				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(writer, "  • %s\n", file)
237			}
238			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
239		}
240	
241		// Show backup info
242		if result.BackupPath != "" {
243			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "💾 Backup created at: %s\n", result.BackupPath)
244			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
245		}

247		return nil

250	func handleConflictOutput(out interface{}, conflicts []ConflictInfo, opts *UpdateOptions) error {
251		writer := out.(io.Writer)
252		red := color.New(color.FgRed)
253		cyan := color.New(color.FgCyan)
254		green := color.New(color.FgGreen)
255	
256		_, _ = red.Fprintln(writer, "⚠️  MERGE CONFLICTS DETECTED")
257		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
258	
259		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(writer, "Found %d conflict(s) that require resolution:\n", len(conflicts))
260		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
261	
262		// Display detailed conflict information
263		for i, conflict := range conflicts {
264			_, _ = red.Fprintf(writer, "❌ Conflict %d: %s (line %d)\n", i+1, conflict.FilePath, conflict.LineNumber)
265			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
266		}
267	
268		// Provide resolution guidance

276		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --strategy=theirs  Accept upstream changes")
277		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --mine             Same as --strategy=ours")
278		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --theirs           Same as --strategy=theirs")
279		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
280		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  🔄 Interactive Resolution:")
281		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --interactive      Resolve conflicts one by one")
282		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
283		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ⚡ Force Resolution:")
284		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --force            Override all conflicts with upstream")
285		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "")
286		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  🔙 Abort Update:")
287		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "    --abort            Cancel update and restore previous state")
288	
289		_, _ = green.Fprintln(writer, "💡 Examples:")

291		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --mine              # Keep all local changes")
292		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --interactive       # Choose per conflict")
293		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(writer, "  ddx update --abort             # Cancel and restore")

295		return fmt.Errorf("conflicts require resolution")

7	# Feature: DDx CLI
8	
9	**ID:** FEAT-001
10	**Status:** In Progress
11	**Priority:** P0
12	**Owner:** DDx Team
13	
14	## Overview
15	
16	The `ddx` CLI is a single Go binary providing all DDx platform services locally: document library management, bead tracker, task execution dispatch, execution definitions and runs, document dependency graph, persona composition, template application, git sync, and meta-prompt injection.
17	
18	## Requirements
19	
20	### Functional
21	
22	**Document Library (implemented)**
23	1. `ddx init` — create `.ddx/library/` structure, generate config, optionally sync from upstream
24	2. `ddx list [type]` — browse documents by category with filtering and JSON output
25	3. `ddx prompts list/show` — browse and inspect AI prompts
26	4. `ddx persona --list/--show/--bind` — manage persona definitions and role
27	   bindings (flag-based interface: `--list`, `--show <name>`, `--bind <name>`,
28	   `--role <name>`, `--tag <name>`)
29	5. ~~`ddx mcp list/install`~~ — **deprecated**. Removed to avoid confusion
30	   with DDx's own MCP server (FEAT-002).
31	5b. ~~`ddx auth`~~ — **deprecated**. Authentication for git subtree removed
32	   alongside subtree itself.
33	6. `ddx doctor` — validate library structure, config, git setup, dependencies
34	7. ~~`ddx update`~~ — **deprecated**. Library sync via git subtree removed.
35	   Content comes from `ddx install`. Run `ddx install <name>` to add resources.
36	8. ~~`ddx contribute`~~ — **deprecated**. Git subtree push removed. Contribute
37	   improvements via PRs to ddx-library or workflow repos directly.
38	9. `ddx upgrade` — self-upgrade binary
39	10. `ddx status` / `ddx log` — show sync state and change history
40	11. Meta-prompt injection into CLAUDE.md during init
41	11b. Project version tracking via `.ddx/versions.yaml` — stamps `ddx_version` on init, gates old binaries from running against newer projects, hints when skills are stale (FEAT-015)
42	
43	**Bead Tracker (implemented — FEAT-004)**
44	12. `ddx bead create/show/update/close` — work item CRUD with `--set key=value` for custom fields
45	13. `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` — query and filter beads

25	## D1 — Policy: library vs project content
26	
27	Personas can live in two places:
28	
29	- **Library personas** — `<library>/personas/*.md`. Shipped with DDx, shared
30	  across projects, pulled via `ddx update`, contributed upstream via
31	  `ddx contribute`. **Read-only in UI and API.** Edits are refused with a
32	  typed error; the only mutation affecting library content is `fork`, which
33	  copies the file into the project.
34	
35	- **Project-local personas** — `.ddx/personas/*.md` under the project root.
36	  Live with the project in its VCS. Fully editable via UI, GraphQL, and CLI.
37	  Never pushed upstream by `ddx contribute`.
38	
39	### Override rule
40	
41	When a project-local persona has the same name as a library persona, the
42	project-local file wins for that project's bindings and list views. List
43	views expose one effective persona per name; the source badge becomes
44	`project` for an override so the reader can see that project-local content
45	is active. Load order: project first, then library; duplicate names collapse
46	to the project-local file.
47	
48	### What this does not decide
49	
50	- Multi-project / org-level persona sharing — out of scope.
51	- Rich in-browser markdown editor — a plain `<textarea>` is enough for v1.
52	- Upstreaming project personas — still happens via `ddx contribute`.
53	- Changing how personas get injected into agent prompts — server read path
54	  is unchanged.
